title: Allow scenario definitions to rerun in the same workspace
description:
Fresh quality review found that scenario run IDs are deterministic from definition name, clock start, organization template, and random seed, while scenario_runs.id is the primary key. Running the same scenario twice in one workspace, or changing event bodies without changing those header fields, collides with the existing run audit row before execution starts. That blocks repeatable learner attempts and makes changed scenario definitions hard to audit.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Parse and run a scenario definition in one SQLite workspace.
2. Run the same definition again in that same workspace.
3. Observe insertScenarioRun fails with a primary-key or unique constraint error on scenario_runs.id.
4. Change the event list while leaving name, clock.start, organization_template, and random_seed unchanged; observe the run ID remains the same.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A scenario can be attempted more than once in the same workspace while preserving durable audit history for each attempt.
- Stable scenario identity and per-attempt run identity are separated, or the run ID includes enough definition content and attempt data to avoid collisions.
- Tests cover rerunning the same definition in one workspace and running two definitions with the same header fields but different event bodies.

title: Close workspace database on unexpected server exit
description:
Fresh quality review found app.Run returns directly from the server.done branch when http.Server.Serve exits before context cancellation. If Start opened a workspace database, that path does not call Server.Close, so the database handle can remain open after an unexpected serve failure.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start the app with a workspace path so Start opens a SQLite database.
2. Force the serving goroutine to exit before the Run context is cancelled, for example by closing the listener or simulating a Serve error.
3. Observe Run returns from the server.done branch without calling Server.Close.
4. The workspace database cleanup path is skipped on that unexpected serve exit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Run closes the workspace database and listener cleanup path on both cancellation and serve-error exits.
- A focused lifecycle test covers the unexpected serve-exit branch.
- The normal graceful-shutdown path still returns the Serve result through Wait without double-closing the database.

title: Snapshot resource tags by usage window, not recording time
description:
Fresh quality review found RecordUsageEvent snapshots whatever resource tags are active at the time the event is recorded. Because activeTags ignores resource_tags.applied_at and removed_at, backfilled usage for a window before a tag was applied can be stored and billed as tagged. This undermines missing/late tag lessons and makes historical billing attribution misleading.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a resource that has no owner tag at 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z.
2. Add owner=platform after that usage window.
3. Record usage for 2026-02-01T00:00:00Z through 2026-02-01T01:00:00Z.
4. Observe usage_events.tag_snapshot_json includes the current owner tag even though the tag was not active during the usage window.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Usage event tag snapshots are selected according to the usage window, not wall-clock recording time.
- Tests cover tags applied after usage began and tags removed before usage.
- Future Cost Explorer and tag activation work can distinguish untagged historical usage from currently tagged resources.

title: Feature: Cost Category rules
description:
Map raw AWS-like costs into business categories.

title: Use the AnyCompany management account as the default billing payer
description:
Fresh quality review found that the resource lab uses defaultAccountID 111122223333 both as the default usage account and as DefaultPayerAccountID. The seeded AnyCompany Retail organization marks 111122223333 as the Storefront Prod member account whose payer_account_id is 999988887777. As a result, the default billing pipeline, daily metering, clock-advance metering, month-end close, and Bills page workflows teach a member-account payer instead of consolidated management-account billing.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a fresh workspace with the AnyCompany Retail fixture.
2. Visit the Resources page and inspect the payer_account_id values prefilled for billing pipeline, daily metering, and month-end close.
3. Observe they default to 111122223333 even though accounts.payer_account_id for that member account is 999988887777.
4. Run the default workflow and observe generated bill line items and closes use the member account as payer unless manually changed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default payer values in resource and bills workflows resolve from the seeded organization management account, or from the selected usage account payer relationship.
- Clock-advance daily metering uses the same resolved payer instead of a hard-coded member account.
- UI tests cover the fresh-workspace default payer and a Storefront Prod usage workflow that bills to 999988887777 by default.

title: Align robot-next tracker commands with br workflow guidance
description:
Fresh quality review found that AGENTS.md and MIND_MAP.md tell agents to use br in this workspace, but bv --robot-next currently emits bd command strings such as bd update and bd show. That creates a process trap at session start: agents following the robot-next JSON literally can violate the local br-only workflow or hit the legacy CLI behavior that MIND_MAP.md already warns about.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run bv --robot-next from the repository root.
2. Observe claim_command and show_command use bd.
3. Compare AGENTS.md, which says the project uses br and lists br ready, br show, br update, br close, and br sync.
4. Compare MIND_MAP.md node [14], which says to use br in this workspace.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Session-start guidance is internally consistent: robot-next output emits br commands, or AGENTS.md documents the exact conversion from bd-form robot output to br commands.
- MIND_MAP.md notes are updated if the expected tracker command source changes.
- A lightweight smoke check or documented verification prevents future guidance from drifting back to contradictory bd/br commands.

title: Validate price catalog consistency

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task description and parent feature constraints.
- Focused test, smoke check, or documented verification exists.
- Result can be exercised in a fresh local workspace or scenario fixture.
notes: Fresh quality review: include canonical effective_date format, currency code strictness, supported-region/unit coverage, and region-code normalization policy in the consistency checks, alongside the existing duplicate-SKU/missing-unit/formula checks.

title: Fix price catalog rate versioning key
description: Fresh quality review found that internal/persistence/migrations/0002_price_catalog_items.sql defines sku as the sole primary key even though PriceCatalogItem is documented as versioned and Lookup selects the latest effective_date <= UsageDate. This prevents adding a later effective date for the same SKU and can force historical rates to masquerade as new SKUs. Acceptance: schema/repository tests prove two rates for the same logical SKU can coexist across effective dates, or the model is deliberately changed so SKU identity is not treated as versioned.

title: Reject ambiguous price catalog lookup dimensions
description: Fresh quality review found lookup selects one row by service_code, usage_type, operation, region_code/global, effective_date DESC, sku, but the schema only enforces SKU uniqueness. Two rows with identical lookup dimensions and effective_date can silently price with whichever SKU sorts first. Acceptance: add a unique constraint or catalog validation for the lookup identity, and test that ambiguous same-date rates fail loudly instead of being selected arbitrarily.

title: Align price catalog zero-rate policy
description: Fresh quality review found the migration allows rate_micros = 0 while TestPriceCatalogSeededForMVPServices treats RateMicros <= 0 as missing price metadata. Decide whether zero-dollar rates belong in the catalog; if not, enforce CHECK (rate_micros > 0) and validation, and if yes, adjust tests and pricing semantics so free-tier or credit rows are modeled deliberately.

title: Split or reject usage windows that cross billing periods
description:
Fresh quality review found billLineItemFromMeteringRecord assigns an entire usage window to the month containing usage_start_time. The existing boundary test expects 2026-02-28T22:00Z through 2026-03-01T02:00Z to stay wholly in February. That will distort monthly bills, Cost Explorer summaries, invoices, and reconciliation unless cross-period usage is split or rejected before pricing.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create an active EC2 resource.
2. Record usage from 2026-02-28T22:00:00Z through 2026-03-01T02:00:00Z with 4 instance hours.
3. Generate metering records and bill line items.
4. Observe a single February billing-period line item for all 4 hours, including the March hours.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Usage that spans a billing period boundary is split into period-contained metering or line-item rows with proportional quantities, or rejected upstream with a clear validation error.
- Boundary tests prove March usage is not silently billed to February.
- Month-end close, summaries, invoices, and exports consume only period-contained billable rows.

title: Use actual billing-period days in resource-lab cost estimates
description:
Fresh quality review found resourceLabHandler.usageEventView prices recent usage with BillingPeriodDays hard-coded to 30. The persisted bill-line-item path uses the actual month length, so resource-lab estimates for GB-day to GB-month storage are wrong in February, leap-year February, March, and any 31-day month.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a storage resource and record one 100 GBDay usage event on 2026-02-01.
2. View the Resources lab Recent Usage estimate.
3. Generate the corresponding bill line item.
4. Compare the UI estimate against persisted pricing; the UI used 30 days while the line-item path used 28 days.

## Acceptance Criteria
- UI estimates derive billing-period days from the usage start date using the same logic as bill line items.
- Tests cover February and 31-day storage estimates matching persisted pricing.
- Any shared billing-period helper avoids duplicating month-length logic between UI and persistence.

title: Generate Support charges from eligible monthly spend
description:
Fresh quality review found the seeded catalog includes AWSSupport with formula eligible_monthly_cost * rate_with_minimum, but the current pricing path only prices individual metering records. There is no period-level job that computes eligible monthly charges, applies the support percentage/minimum rule, and emits a support bill line item. This can mislead later Bills and Cost Explorer work because the catalog advertises Support while the engine cannot derive it correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Support pricing is generated from finalized or estimated eligible monthly spend, not from ad hoc user usage events.
- The synthetic percentage and minimum rule is implemented deliberately and documented in tests.
- Ineligible line item types are excluded from the eligible-spend base.
- Reconciliation tests prove support totals trace back to eligible source line items.

title: Reject usage outside resource lifecycle windows
description:
Fresh quality review found ResourceUsageRepository.RecordUsageEvent only checks service match and the usage window shape. It does not reject usage for planned resources, usage before started_at, usage after stopped_at or deleted_at, or generated patterns for resources that are not active during the requested dates. The UI exposes planned, stopped, and deleted resource states, so impossible charges can enter metering and line items.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a resource with status planned and no started_at.
2. Record usage for that resource with any valid usage window.
3. Observe the usage event is accepted and can later be metered and priced.
4. Repeat with a stopped resource and a usage window after stopped_at; observe the same acceptance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Repository validation enforces resource lifecycle windows for manual and generated usage.
- Tests cover planned resources, before-start windows, after-stop windows, and after-delete windows.
- Any allowed stopped-resource charges are explicitly bounded through the stop timestamp.

title: Report created versus reused generated usage events
description:
Fresh quality review found GenerateUsage uses deterministic IDs and ON CONFLICT(id) DO NOTHING, then returns the existing rows. That makes reruns idempotent at the database level, but the result only exposes Events and the UI says Generated N usage events even when a rerun inserted zero new rows.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create an S3 resource.
2. Generate the requests pattern for 3 days.
3. Generate the same pattern again for the same resource and date range.
4. Observe the database row count is unchanged, but the result and UI still report the full generated event count.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Generation results expose created and reused counts, or equivalent status.
- The resource-lab flash distinguishes no-op reruns from new inserts.
- Tests cover repeated generation reporting without increasing usage_events.

title: Prevent payer mismatches from closing bills with stranded line items
description:
Fresh quality review found that line items are permanently keyed to the payer used when pricing runs. If daily metering or the manual billing pipeline prices usage with one payer, a later month-end close for a different payer sees the metering records as already priced, creates no replacement line items for the requested payer, and can issue a zero or incomplete bill while the original estimated line items remain open under the other payer. This is especially risky now that AnyCompany member accounts have a separate management payer.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create usage for account 111122223333 in a fresh workspace.
2. Run daily metering or the billing pipeline with payer_account_id 111122223333 so estimated bill_line_items are created for the member payer.
3. Advance the simulator clock past the billing period and close the same period with payer_account_id 999988887777.
4. Observe the close succeeds for the management payer with zero or missing finalized usage, because the metering records were already consumed by member-payer line items.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Month-end close refuses payer-mismatched prepriced line items with an actionable error, or reassigns/reprices them through an explicit consolidated-billing rule.
- Tests cover a member-payer daily metering run followed by a management-payer close.
- Bills, reconciliation, and invoice totals cannot silently omit usage because the payer chosen during earlier estimated pricing differs from the close payer.
